utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 items. Now check completeness: (1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan provides both physical_health and mental_health with providers, policy numbers, monthly costs, and coverage descriptions—fully specified with costs. (2) property_coverage: home/contents insurance with costs. The plan lists provider, policy number, monthly cost, and coverage description—fully specified with costs. (3) travel_coverage: travel insurance with costs. The plan lists provider, policy number, monthly cost, and coverage description—fully specified with costs. (4) additional_services: any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists several services but does not include costs for these services; the field explicitly asks for services with costs, so this is not fully specified. (5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan includes monthly cost, annual cost, and a breakdown—fully specified. Thus, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four selectable items that correspond to entries in the ground-truth ratings: InsurePlus Standard Plan (used for both physical and mental health), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (property), and PrimeTravel Premium + Business Travel Support (travel). In the ground-truth, InsurePlus Standard Plan has ratings for both physical health (6) and mental health (6). PrimeHome Premium Coverage has a rating of 10. For travel, the closest match to 'PrimeTravel Premium + Business Travel Support' is the base 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' rated 10; 'Business Travel Support' appears under travel enhancement services with a rating of 10, but since the coverage plan lists it as part of the travel provider name, we map the core 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' to its rating of 10.
